topic: Testing harness tools — multi-layer testing of LLM-facing agent tools
notes: Specialized for @agent-sh/harness-* (TypeScript library shipping LLM-facing tools). Confidence is high for live-fetched external sources and in-repo anchors; moderate for secondary sources that redirected or returned partial content.

sources.title: Anthropic — Define success criteria and build evaluations
keyInsights: Success criteria must be specific, measurable, achievable, relevant
Three grading methods: code-based (fastest, most reliable), human (expensive), LLM-based (flexible, scalable after validation)
LLM-based grading rubrics should encourage reasoning in <thinking> tags then discard, use strict small-scale outputs (correct/incorrect or 1-5 Likert)
Evaluate with a different model than the one under test to avoid self-grading bias
Volume over quality for automated evals; more signal from many medium-quality cases than few human-graded

sources.title: Anthropic — Raising the bar on SWE-bench Verified
keyInsights: Minimal scaffold: bash + edit tool only, give max control to the model
Explicitly 'tested them to uncover any ways that the model might misunderstand the spec' and iterated descriptions
Forced absolute paths to prevent relative-path errors after cd
String replacement edits found to have highest reliability after experimenting with alternatives
Scaffolding iteration, not the model, drove the score from 45% to 49%

sources.title: Anthropic — How we built our multi-agent research system
keyInsights: Built a tool-testing agent that attempts the tool and rewrites the description to avoid failures
Description improvements cut future-agent task time by 40%
Full production tracing for systematic failure diagnosis; token usage alone explains 80% of variance in BrowseComp
Evaluations must tolerate multiple valid paths to the same goal — strict trajectory equality is wrong
Human review caught subtle failure modes (source quality bias) that automated systems missed

sources.title: Claude Code — Best Practices
keyInsights: Highest-leverage pattern: give the model a way to verify its own work (tests, screenshots, scripts)
Context-window exhaustion is the dominant cause of performance degradation
Hooks are deterministic guarantees; CLAUDE.md instructions are advisory
Treat CLAUDE.md like code: review, prune, test behavior shifts when rules are added
Failure patterns: kitchen-sink session, repeated corrections, over-specified CLAUDE.md, trust-then-verify gap

sources.title: Anthropic — Tool use with Claude (overview)
keyInsights: strict:true on tool definitions guarantees schema conformance; costs extra tokens
Claude Opus is more likely than Sonnet to ask for missing required parameters rather than hallucinate
Tool-use system-prompt token counts vary by model (346 on 4.x, 264 on Haiku 3.5, 530 on Opus 3)
tool_choice: auto|any|tool|none — differs in token accounting
Server tools (web_search, code_execution) execute on Anthropic infra; client tools stream tool_use → tool_result

sources.title: Anthropic — Project Vend: Claude as a shopkeeper
keyInsights: Long-horizon autonomous agent exposed emergent failures not visible in short-context evals
Concrete failure modes: hallucinated payment accounts, under-cost pricing, over-discounting, ignoring high-value offers
Identity-crisis episode demonstrated context-accumulation instability
Observability via financial record tracking enabled systematic diagnosis of economic outcomes
Better scaffolding (structured reflection tools, CRM) likely addresses many failures — confirms scaffold-iteration as primary lever

sources.title: Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard (BFCL)
keyInsights: AST-based validation for syntax + executable evaluation for actual callability
Categories: simple, parallel, multiple, multi_turn, live_multiple, web_search, memory
First comprehensive executable function-call eval — not just format-conformance
Distinguishes Live (single-turn) from Non-Live categories
Format sensitivity is an explicit evaluation axis

sources.title: TAU-bench — simulated user + agent + tools
keyInsights: Three-way interactions: user simulator + agent + domain tools (unlike BFCL which is tool-only)
Pass@k (Pass¹ Pass² Pass³ Pass⁴) for explicit reliability scoring
Airline + retail domains with policy constraints
Auto-error identification tools for fault classification (agent fault vs user fault vs policy violation)
Compares across closed and open models on realistic multi-turn flows

sources.title: SWE-bench — Can LLMs resolve real-world GitHub issues?
keyInsights: Input = codebase + issue; Output = patch; Score = pass/fail on existing test suite
Variants: Lite (subset), Verified (500 human-confirmed-solvable), Multimodal
Evaluator uses Docker for reproducibility — patch applied in container, test suite runs
Functional correctness over code-similarity metrics
Benchmark validates tool-using agent capability because solving requires navigation + understanding + editing

sources.title: Inspect AI — UK AISI eval framework
keyInsights: Three-part model: Datasets + Solvers + Scorers
Solvers are chained components (prompt engineering + elicitation strategies)
Scorers: text match, model-graded, or custom
Built-in tools: bash, Python, text editing, web search, browsing, computer
Sandboxing: Docker, Kubernetes, Modal, Proxmox — for untrusted model code
Supports running external agents (Claude Code, Gemini CLI) inside eval tasks

sources.title: Promptfoo — Anthropic provider docs
keyInsights: YAML config for side-by-side model comparison across providers
Tool configuration via inline input_schema + name + description
Model-graded assertions: llm-rubric, factuality
Structured outputs incompatible with citations + message prefilling
strict:true for schema conformance on tool calls

sources.title: OpenAI Evals — framework for LM evaluations
keyInsights: Open-source registry of benchmarks + custom eval authoring
Completion Function Protocol for prompt chains and tool-using agents
Data fetched via Git-LFS for select evals
Eval templates pattern — contributors follow existing shapes
Framework is LM-or-system agnostic (not OpenAI-specific)

sources.title: DeepEval — LLM-app evaluation in pytest style
keyInsights: ToolCorrectness metric — did right tools get called with right args
ArgumentCorrectness metric — validates tool-call arguments specifically
TaskCompletion metric — did the agent accomplish its goal
StepEfficiency — unnecessary steps penalty
Plan{Adherence,Quality} — multi-step plan evaluation
LLMTestCase + @observe decorator; end-to-end or component-level

sources.title: LangSmith — evaluation concepts
keyInsights: Pairwise evaluators compare two application versions (heuristic, LLM, or human judge)
Dataset splits = named subsets; versions auto-created on example change
Manually curated 10-20 examples covering common + edge cases as starting point
Pairwise useful 'when directly scoring an output is difficult but comparing is straightforward'
Explicit mention of correct-tool-selection and argument-formatting as eval targets

sources.title: MCP Inspector — dev tool for testing MCP servers
keyInsights: React UI + Node proxy bridging browser to MCP servers via various transports
Form-based tool execution with real-time response visualization
Defers deep validation to the server; omits optional fields when empty
CLI mode for programmatic / scripted server testing
Request history + visual error display — useful for debugging tool-description mismatches

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK — Tracing docs
keyInsights: Traces comprise hierarchical spans: LLM generations, tool calls, handoffs, guardrails, custom events
function_span() wraps every tool call, stores inputs/outputs (toggleable for privacy)
Tracing enabled by default, disableable via env / code / per-run
Platform dashboard for visualizing complete workflow structure
Sensitive-data capture is an explicit configurable dimension

sources.title: VCR.py — record/replay HTTP interactions
keyInsights: YAML cassettes capture request + response pairs per test context
Replay phase intercepts recognized requests, returns recorded response — no network call
Benefits: offline capability, determinism, speed, zero API cost
Cassette invalidation = delete file and re-record; maintenance is lightweight
Applicable pattern for LLM APIs (harness regression tests), but doesn't catch description regressions

sources.title: Hermes Function Calling — open-weights tool-use format
keyInsights: <tool_call>{json}</tool_call> XML-wrapped JSON as the expected output format
validator.py + Pydantic schema enforcement for output validation
template_tests directory for structured test fixtures
Recursive iteration handling with a max-depth parameter
Format-correctness focus over accuracy quantification in public docs

sources.title: Vitest API reference — test.runIf, test.each, test.concurrent
keyInsights: test.runIf(condition)(name, fn) and test.skipIf(condition)(name, fn) for env-gated tests
test.each([...cases])(name, fn) with printf-style %s/%d/%i/%j formatting
test.for([...]) — like each but doesn't spread; provides TestContext for concurrent snapshots
test.concurrent(name, fn, timeout?) — consecutive tests parallelized; use { expect } from context for snapshot correctness
Per-test timeout: 3rd arg (number) or options object { timeout }

sources.title: In-repo anchor — existing test infrastructure
keyInsights: Trace-capturing helper collectTrace() records turns, toolsByName, toolSeq, finalContent, full events
runE2E(opts) dispatches on resolveBackend() (ollama | bedrock) — test bodies are backend-agnostic
it.runIf(() => available) + beforeAll probe (ollamaModelAvailable / bedrockAvailable) gates real-model tests
Per-test timeouts 180_000 / 240_000 / 300_000 ms passed as third arg, not global
Bash-decoy tests (H7, W8) surface tool-bypass as console.warn, not test failure (drift signal without CI red)
InMemoryLedger + recordRead() helper drives read-before-mutate gate + STALE_READ tests
Discriminated-union result kinds (text | directory | attachment | error) with specific error codes for LLM recovery
makeSession() with DEFAULT_SENSITIVE_PATTERNS + permission hook variants covers fail-open-to-hook vs hard-deny (D11)
Bedrock gated by loadDotEnv() + AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK; zero-dep .env loader walks up from cwd
E2E_BACKEND + BEDROCK_MODEL_ID env switches backend without touching test code
